items.comment:
Nice place, great location.
However, if you are uncomfortable with homeless people literally sleeping up against the house, laying on the entrance way, asking you for drugs as you walk in/out of garage, and defecating on the driveway, this is not the place for you. Be aware, it is Venice, after all.
Other than that, the place was fine.
Property manager, Andrew (not the owner), who interacted with us on site was kind and attentive. Would have felt more like a proper Airbnb experience had we been hosted by the actual host / owner, but not a huge deal. Either way, Andrew promptly so
items.one_line: Homeless defecation on driveway, neighbor's feces tips

items.comment: Not as advertised in my opinion. We had to leave after the first night, mostly because of the noise. I could go on and on about a slew of vitriolic texts from Neil and Katie, including "You have an ugly personality," an "ugly nature," and "Grow up and get lost. Thanks for your $$!" Bottom line: this place is BEYOND LOUD at night. I’m not talking cool, ambient city noise. We live in a huge city with nightly helicopters, etc. We also love the West Village, having stayed there countless times, including on Horatio, 8th, and 10th. So *believe me* when I tell you this level of noise is un-freaking-
items.one_line: Live music club blasts til 3am, host calls guests ugly, insults

items.comment:
**WARNING DANGEROUS AND UNSAFE CONDITIONS - DETAILS BELOW**
[TLDR - 24 hour "security guard" was sleeping while I was beaten and robbed out front of the building]
Long Version:
I stayed for 3 of 5 days of my reservation at this listing. It was clean and the listing was accurate. However, on the third night I returned home and there were 3 men waiting in front of the building smoking.
**When I approached I was attacked and robbed.**
I resisted and much as I could, and the attack dragged on for about 1 & 1/2 minutes. There are cameras and a 24 hour guard, so I assumed if
items.one_line: Attacked and robbed outside, security guard was sleeping

items.comment:
let me post the original dialogue
Elaine said: Hi, I write to let you know that the other airbnb guest for our house is extremely noisy and dirty. Nobody had been able to sleep for a few days and that they hardly flush the toilet or wash the dishes. Things had been so bad that even neighbors called the police the night before last night however things did not change last night and I am receiving complaints first thing in the morning. They will still be here during your stay. We are negotiating with them urgently to try to persuade them to leave but have not see any positive response so far
items.one_line: Noisy guest, police called, free house with no utilities

items.comment: When me and my friends were looking for a place to stay. We listed few amenities that we needed. Also used filter. When we found Alex's place, we decided to stay as it had definitely nice view and met all the listed amenities including parking lot. However, after the first night of stay, our car has disappeared from the parking lot, without any notes. This was terrifying because we thought the car has been stolen. Alex and us called the building manager, however he CCTV records were all deleted. Then, we called cops and etc. so we tracked out vehicle with the help of police and found that is h
items.one_line: Car towed from parking lot, CCTV deleted, guest used as evidence collector

items.comment:
We stayed in the Guesthouse from January-March while completing one of my clinical rounds. I was not really around much given that, but my husband used the guesthouse as a workplace the majority of the time.

It is important to note a few things: There are multiple rentals at this property with duplicate photos. There are some photos of the outside of the guesthouse, but no other accurate representations of the inside other than the kitchen sink. The photos imply that there are multiple rooms, a bedroom with bed and separate living area. However, the guesthouse is a studio-loft with
items.one_line: Photos show main house, guest receives studio loft on floor

items.comment: We came to check in at this property around 9:30PM. We arrived at the property, the first impression from us were why does the house look like a haunted house. We opened the front door, the light was not working properly, it was flashing on and off (disappointing), we went into each rooms which causes me to feel more uncomfortable because all the pillows cover sheet were smelling like cigarette. I could easily spot spider nets up on the wall everywhere. Then I found a spider on the wall which got me frozen because it was a huge spider that I’ve never seen in my life, I had to tried to kill it
items.one_line: Haunted house flashing lights, giant spider, cockroaches

items.comment:
Do not stay in this place. Hieu's property may be lovely in the pictures, but up close it is poorly maintained, disgustingly dirty, and not well cared for. What's more, the host has been shown to be petty and vindictive, engaging in retaliatory behavior and harassment.
When I went there for my wife's 40th birthday party, as part of a surprise I spent a year organizing, it was one thing to have to clean the place, but quite another to have to deal with a 10ft - 15ft fire that started because the grease trap on the grill had not been emptied in months or perhaps all summer. Two of my guest
items.one_line: Dirty property, grease trap fire threatens guests, host retaliates with harassment

items.comment: Our experience with Nath and the entire booking was the WORST we had on Airbnb. The story goes as follows: - We arrived to the apartment for check-in at around 8 pm in the evening for a long 42 nights' stay, Nath was not there to welcome us or give us a tour of the apartment, but she sent her friend "Ginette" to give us the keys. Ginette was a nice lady who quickly explained the "recycling" and "house keys" rules of the apartment and left us to our stay. - That same night at around midnight, my wife and I were looking to go to bed for a well-deserved rest after our 24 hour flight, and were loo
items.one_line: Guest accidentally triggers panic button, host checks up constantly

items.comment:
Lia is a gracious host. She was very prompt with communication and very accommodating. When it became clear during the first night that the arrangement would not be adequate for us to continue there (see below) and we cancelled our remaining nights, she graciously returned her second night's charges even though we cancelled a few hours too late to be reimbursed automatically by airBnB according to the refund policy on Lia's page.
The reason for our cancellation during the first night was the level of noise. The description does include the phrase "with airplanes reminding us that we live
items.one_line: Lisbon apartment under airport runway descent path literally

items.comment:
A spectacular view of Paris from a large terrace, shame about the cockroach infested apartment and unwarranted flooding accusations by the owner.
On the third evening of our six night stay the first cockroaches appeared and their numbers increased each evening. We dreaded returning to the flat as there was no relaxation or sleep until we had spent an hour or more killing off the cockroaches.
Clearly not a pleasant way to end a day spent in Paris. We informed the owner immediately and she admitted that it was an on going problem within the building. However, after following up with pho
items.one_line: Host accuses guests of bringing cockroaches to Paris apartment

items.comment:
What Nightmares Are Made Of ...
This was the worst experience of my entire life, from start to finish. I booked a stay in this apartment from Aug 28 - Oct 2 to facilitate my move to Paris for study. The host Carline bullied me and forced me to leave because I had fallen asleep with a lamp on.
The first red flag was when she sent me a derrogatory message when I requested to stay, questioning whether or not I was a woman and demanding that I confirm this and making remarks about my name. I was insulted however, as a 23 year old student, my income is very limited so I decided to proceed
items.one_line: Host bullies guest out for sleeping with lamp on

items.comment:
It does what it says on the tin, but feels a bit misleading anyway. Will do in a pinch but not ideal for a nice holiday.
Minor inconveniences added up:
1) Noisy neighbours
2) Sewage and marijuana smell
3) "Balcony" door doesn't close properly and main door lets hallway light in
4) Have desklamps but missing lightbulbs
5) Have shower but showerhead is broken
6) Have internet but stutters even for one user watching Netflix
7) Have TV but no channels only subscription services
8) Have kitchen but no cutlery or dishrag
9) Have balcony but in the basement
items.one_line: Have balcony but it's basement surrounded by walls on all sides

items.comment: This home is not somewhere you want to take your family.  Upon pulling up to the home, you see an overgrown front yard with long and weed infested yard.  Upon entering:  It was filthy with cobwebs everywhere, both inside and out.  You looked out the windows and saw filth and cobwebs on all windows.  You then tried to open blinds, only to find cobwebs and dirt on them.  The kitchen had a cracked tile right in the middle, with a rug thrown over it.  When Chelsea was questioned about it, she told us "it adds character to the house".  It was unsafe and felt like we had to walk around this all the
items.one_line: Cracked tile "adds character"; cobwebs, no closets, bed toppers

items.comment: It wasn’t horrible considering we booked it so late and had nothing else to book but there was a makeup hand print on our wall, the door didn’t close unless we locked it, there was a mouse hole stuffed with like tissue and tape and it was quite dirty and outdated but it did the trick. Also, it was absurdly loud due to the location and we watched a huge rat eat a mouse right outside the bnb when we were walking to the nearby store, my boyfriend was not phased but it most certainly freaked me out after seeing the patched mouse hole lol. However, he was a very nice host and very easy to contact.
items.one_line: Rat ate mouse outside; tissue-stuffed mouse hole inside

items.comment: This is a wonderful apartment located on the beautiful Parque México in Condesa. The location is simply unbeatable. The complimentary breakfast is convenient; a great time to people/dog watch whilst fueling up for the day. Our first two nights of our stay were great. HOWEVER, we then had an extremely upsetting experience on the third night which ruined our trip. After a busy day/night touring about the city, we arrived back to the Airbnb at midnight, absolutely exhausted, only to find ANOTHER PERSON OCCUPYING OUR ROOM, blasting music, smoking, and clearly on drugs. In short, this person had re
items.one_line: Stranger on drugs steals bedroom, blasts music, smokes

items.comment: Good location, parking easy. Inside very dark with dim lighting, place has a musty smell. Beds a bit uncomfortable. All of this as expected I suppose. However, the toilet had major issues for the first half of our stay. We plunged it multiple times, Jerry did arrange for a plumber but that didn't completely resolve the issue. The issue appeared to be old pipes in the building. Ultimately we went next door to the drug store to poop and never used any toilet paper in the toilet and were mostly able to flush the toilet. This isn't really Jerry's fault but still impacted our stay in a major way an
items.one_line: Guest poops next door, host ignores discount request for plunging toilet

items.comment:
Listing mensonger!! Misleading listing, if not a scam!
This is definitely not a villa, more like a shack or maybe a studio with a mezzanine and two tiny rooms built out of an old garage !
The upstairs room has a very little area where adults could not stand up without hitting the ceiling, also there is no door and you can see the kitchen from the bathtub.
Coffee machine was listed however there was a sign asking for $1 per cup, as well as snack for sell in the fridge. This is not the Airbnb experience we have experience ever, especially for 300+ per night !!!
The worst part ma
items.one_line: Villa actually a garage with $1 coffee machine

items.comment: I would love to write a stellar review of this apartment, managed by Del Parque Flats, but unfortunately, each of my hopeless-believer-in-second-third-and-even-fourth-chances efforts to provide an occasion to write such a review was met with a slammed door. Or, rather, a door that was impossible to slam (keep reading this review if you’d like to understand this joke). From a basic usability standpoint, this apartment was not unlike an abandoned waterpark, a frightening mess top to bottom. Similar to my joke above, the punchline was delivered as a punch that leaves one feeling depressed and tir
items.one_line: Apartment like abandoned waterpark, door won't slam

items.comment: The place and the location was fine.  However this was the worst airbnb experience in my life. Flavio threatened me not to write this review with the consequences of trashing me - but I finally decided to review . - My husband and I arrived late at 10pm to his place in Roma from Ohio. It was Easy to find, well located, and clean. After getting in our pajamas, we decided to go out on the courtyard balcony. The door locked behind us with all money, passports and phones inside. An American couple was outside and let us use their phone to call Airbnb. Flavio said he was far away and for us to go t
items.one_line: Locked out at 10pm, spent night outside freezing

items.comment: We felt extremely unsafe in the street at night because the apartment is located in a deprived area where all kinds of shady characters are hanging around, smoking weed and a garbage dump with rats. We knew the location was in an nightlife area, but the noise pollution was extreme. There is a nightclub right below the apartment that plays loud music until 5 am that you hear so loudly that it feels like you are in the club! We tried to sleep on the couch in the living room, but the nuisance was also very bad there. The host has provided earplugs, thank you, but the walls are so thin that you ar
items.one_line: Nightclub below plays until 5am, feels like sleeping in club

items.comment:
This cabin has many issues, but there are two which make me think it should not be used.
There is a light switch on the bathroom wall, which I believe is illegal. Under the draining board, which is made of wooden slats, with wide gaps, is an electric plug outlet, with an extension lead with kettle and toaster connected. Any liquid spill could cause injury or death. I am not a professional electrician but I consider these issues vitally important.
Other things included rather amateur plumbing, some of it on the floor, no heating (but a Honeywell controller on table), CO2 detector
items.one_line: Light switch in bathroom, electrical outlet under sink, dangerous

items.comment:
Do not stay here if you want to sleep. the location is on top of a bar which has VERY loud music all night (until 7am on Fri and Sat) with a bass beat which vibrates the floors of the whole house. Earplugs are provided but especially on a warm night you cannot open windows to get a breeze. The apartment is very central and spacious in general but one of the bedrooms is only accessible through the other one and there is one bathroom with a small sink, shower and toilet. Our group wouldn't have minded that but as we didn't sleep, we were quite exhausted.
Casper provided some extra glasses
items.one_line: Above bar with bass vibrating floors until 7am on weekends

items.comment: We spent 4 nights in mid-June and were absolutely pleased with Nuno's flats (there are 2, back to back). Although small, ours was intelligently laid out with almost everything placed where you needed it. Bright and airy. It felt right the moment we stepped in the door. The kitchen has a view of the city to the west, while the bedroom looks out east. And the terrace...!! I've been around most of the world's continents and can safely say that I do not remember ever having had a city view from a lodging with such a sweep. A panorama of more than 320 degrees. You can have breakfast and watch the s
items.one_line: Peacock barks like monkey in heat despite amazing terrace view

items.comment: Disclaimer: the space itself was perfectly fine. It was very clean and cozy, the host communicated very well, the view of the streets is beautiful, and we were even welcomed with a bottle of wine to congratulate us on our honeymoon with a sweet note from the host. We would have liked to give a higher review but unfortunately, our stay was ruined by loud prevailing noise from the upstairs neighbors that occurred every night but one during our stay. Some noise is understandable especially when staying in an apartment building, but this wasn’t occasional, and it wasn’t music or party noise, but s
items.one_line: Honeymoon ruined by upstairs sex sounds hourly all night

items.comment:
SPIDERS IN BEDS, SPIDER NETS, LOUD/BROKEN BEDROOM DOORS, AND DIRTY AIR VENTS ARE NOT ACCEPTABLE and you as a host unwilling to do anything this situation is even worse! After traveling all day and being completely tired, we arrived to Frank's place thinking we could finally rest and go to sleep but really what we discovered inside our beds what HORROR and nothing desirable by any means.
We walked into the place and a gave a bowl of water to our dog and went straight to the bedrooms, upon opening one of the bedroom doors or should I say dragging the bedroom door against the cement floors (
items.one_line: Spiders in beds and sheets greet exhausted travelers

items.comment:
PLEASE READ BEFORE YOU BOOK!!
My stay here was a nightmare. I was traveling with my partner and our ESA, just looking for a place to lay our heads for a few days, so our standards were already low. But this unit was absolutely DISGUSTING. The first thing i noticed was the difference in quality of the room compared to the pictures. There are things shown on the pictures that did not translate in person, for example the back of the entrance door is really dirty and the paint is scratched all over, the toilet paper holder is broken off, the hair dryer is so old that it does not work, and the
items.one_line: Sheets covered in sand, dust, hair including animal hairs

items.comment:
I and my wife were not satisfied with the house. Firstly, the next house was turned on the music too loudly all night even while we were sleeping, also we woke up because of the too loud music.
Secondly, the mattress of the bed in the room is the worst one that I have in my life. We couldn't sleep well because of the mattress, and we had back pain the day after. It felt like water or air mattress.
The worst thing that I had was the host asked us money after we left. My wife started her period abruptly, and she so embarrassed. So she tried to use everything carefully. But we needed to
items.one_line: Host calls repeatedly at 11pm demanding toilet be cleaned

items.comment: The room DID look like the photos. But the SMELL was horrible. Had to by febreze. Treacherous stairs up to room. Almost fell several times. Dirty/shabby common areas. Gross toilet, oven, and refrigerators. Fire alarm chirped the majority of my stay. Found blood spots and dirt on my bedding before I slept in it suggesting it wasn't changed between guests. Had to reuse (questionable) towels - I was only given two. House was hard to find - had to ask a neighbor. LOUD neighborhood with screaming & fights & music blasting outside. No fan, so the room was very hot and opening the window made everyth
items.one_line: Blood on sheets, fire alarm chirping, murder history nearby

items.comment:
I’ve been traveling through Mexico for 9 months and this was by far the best airbnb I’ve stayed in so far! Do not hesitate to rent this place!!
Let’s start with the negative things to get them out of the way: any noise from the street comes into the bedroom as there are single pane windows. It’s typically a very quiet street, but if you’re a light sleeper, I would recommend ear plugs for sure! There’s a gentleman who starts sweeping the street at 5:30 am almost every day and for some reason, it’s very loud sweeping and repeatedly woke me up until I started using the earplugs. Also, random
items.one_line: Street sweeper's 5:30am aggressive sweeping defeats all sleep attempts

items.comment:
This was one of my worst AirBnB stays but absolutely not due to the host because he’s says he was unaware of construction.
The building next door was majorly damaged in the 2017 earthquake and is being demolished and rebuilt. Starting between 7-7:30 am every single morning but Sunday, the workers start jack hammering, sawing and blasting music. This all goes on right behind the wall where the bed is los Ted and floats through the large back windows overlooking a shared parking lot/courtyard. The noise goes until between 5:30-7 pm at night. It is impossible to sleep through, difficult to h
items.one_line: Earthquake rebuilding jackhammers destroy sanity for two years straight

items.comment:
Very good location, but it is NOT quiet. I have years of experience with long term stays in CDMX, this was the worst place I've stayed, and that includes a place that had to be fumigated for cockroaches, twice!
The apartment faces into an open space with other apartments, you will be part of their conversations, fights, meetings, and the incessantly barking dog that the owner does not stop. The building is new, but of very poor quality construction: you will hear the snoring above, the vibrating alarm clock, and when they have sex. You will hear the neighbours conversing next door, and
items.one_line: Worse than cockroach fumigation due to relentless neighbor noise

items.comment: Meeting Jonathan went great, seems like a nice young man. Met in front of his place the first night. A prior message from me stated that I worked every day of my stay, 7 days a week, 12 hours a day. I’m a very quiet individual and keep to myself. I’m clean and respect everyone’s property and keep my noise to a respectful level, if not, more quiet than the average person to avoid any intrusion on anyones sleep or daily schedule. I informed Jonathan upon seeing him my first evening stay that I worked in the executive protection field/Private Investigation field as a owner/operator for high profi
items.one_line: Host parties loudly above sleeping executive protection professional

items.comment:
Full disclosure: the conditions of the apartment related to our comfort were so terrible, that me and my girlfriend of two years fought a lot and eventually broke up. We were fighting loudy for at least 5 days out of our 20 days of stay (never during the night to respect the other guests) and the receptionist were patient and kind enough. I apologize for the inconvenience caused. Hopefully you saw how respectful we were towards the employees.
Apart from this, the apartment was a mess at the beginning. The room was clearly not done. Beds were ok, no towels, room was messy and dirty all thor
items.one_line: Terrible apartment conditions cause couple to break up mid-stay

items.comment:
I WOULD NOT advise anyone to stay here.
We had a very bad experience to the point we actually had to source and pay for other accommodation during our stay in vienna.
We arrived to the property around 11.30 pm at night, got the keys fine from the instructions (which are very thorough) but felt very uncomfortable as soon as we entered. Firstly we were approached by several people hanging around the gate, offering strange comments and as two girls on their own new to vienna, it wasn’t a nice experience. The place from the exterior is metal gates and my freind described it as a prison l
items.one_line: Screaming neighbors, broken lock, rented other accommodation

items.comment: We had to carry our luggage up to the third floor apartment and were thoroughly exhausted from the exercise. We found out later that there was an easier but unadvertised way. We left the place for a hotel after four days even though we had paid for eight days mainly because we did not have a telephone that could be used in Vienna. We were astonished that the apartment did not have a land-line telephone. Since I had to reach Lufthansa immediately because of a suitcase that did not arrive with us, I had to go down to the street and ask a lady walking her dog to let me use her phone. The brother
items.one_line: No phone, host blamed guest for not knowing unlisted requirement

items.comment:
Constance herself is super nice and very sweet. However, her husband made me feel extremely uncomfortable.
When I first got there, he checked me in and showed me my room. He proceeded to closing the door with the two of us in there. I was frightened and quickly asked where the bathroom was so he could leave the bedroom.
On the second day, I left my room to use the bathroom and he opened the door on me. He didn't knock or apologize and the bathroom door doesn't lock well so every time I was in the bathroom, I was concerned the door would open again.
In addition, the halls are dark
items.one_line: Husband closed door menacingly, turned off bathroom light

items.comment:
Dead cockroaches, dirty clothes (dirty sock in bathroom and unknown pair of trousers in wardrobe) dirty dishes. dirty smelly cloths and rags. Dusty, black, thick dirt and dead bus everywhere early not hoovered or cleaned properly in weeks - or even checked. Bathroom had dirty used earbud and toilet wasn't the cleanest.
Host let us drop bags off an hour early but then said the place was ready. Gave allowance for that but as we spent more time there became clear it hadn't been cleaned in weeks not a result of having an hour less to clean.
This place has little to no essentials. Two tea
items.one_line: Dead cockroaches, dirty socks, and weeks of uncleaned filth.

items.comment:
I'm going to be as objective and fair as possible, which is somewhat difficult given that we were robbed at gunpoint literally on the steps leading into this apartment.

To back up, my girlfriend and I (both in our early 30s) stayed in this apartment between Dec. 30, 2013 and Jan. 3, 2014. The apartment is exactly as advertised and Connie was very responsive with us leading up to our stay at the apartment. I echo everyone else's reviews in commending her for the extra touches like having snacks, water, etc. The place also was very clean.

The only negative I had about
items.one_line: Robbed at gunpoint on apartment steps, host very responsive

items.comment: We read all the positive reviews for this place and while the host tries her best many thing felt short. We always inspect the sheets when we get somewhere and one of the sheet was full of blood specs and someone had written on it in greek with a blue pen. The bathroom is scary, there is a wooden plank on the roof of where the bowl is. You can see through the cracks to the upstairs neighbours. You can definitively hear them so they can probably hear you on the bowl. The water from the shower does have a lot of hot water but it is not constant, one moment it will be burning hot then icy cold th
items.one_line: Bloody sheets, see-through bathroom walls, scalding shower temperature

items.comment:
Well the view is awesome and the location is unbeatable. But our experience beyond that was soured very quickly and we ended up having to leave shortly after arriving.
We put our stuff down and marveled at the view out of the window. Every time we washed our hands in the bathroom we noticed a small leak on the ground but decided to ignore that… we went to dinner and came back and I decided to take a shower. The host let us know that the shower is dangerously hot so to be careful. I turned on the shower and waited truly a few seconds to get in so I could get a sense of the temperature. The
items.one_line: Shower head falls off, power outage, host doesn't respond to messages

items.comment:
My family and I booked a 28days stay at this listing. My parents, slept in the main room with bathroom. They arrived first from Cabo. My husband and I, we arrived a week after from the US and slept in the 2nd room.
Unfortunately, I am giving this place overall of 2/5 stars, with these breakdowns:
1. Cleanliness- 1/5. the first day of arrival, both bathrooms smelled like sewer. The lobby also smelled like sewer when we arrived, so we thought maybe they are cleaning the septic tank? the smell was so potent so we ended up mopping and washing both bathrooms and turned on the fans. The couc
items.one_line: Sewer smell, bed bugs, rashes: perfect family vacation

items.comment: ROACHES AHEAD. The location of this place is prime. Literally steps from the boardwalk and beach, close to restaurants and shops. There is a full size kitchen with everything you need to prepare meals. Full size stove and refrigerator. There is a pool on premises which is a nice extra. Besides this, nothing else good to mention. There is Roku on all TVs, but it’s locked and YouTube is not one the channels in the list. So if you would like to watch YouTube, you would have to do it on your phone. I did contact the owner about this and their response, more than 24 hr  later was it’s locked, no so
items.one_line: Fire alarm rings every thirty seconds, owner slow to respond overall

items.comment: the shower was dangerous and unusable for the first two nights. it only operated at seemingly the hottest temperature possible, and we could not figure out how to cool it down. on the first night, while we tried to figure it out, it got so hot that the smoke detector went off several times, making for an intensely hectic first night. we thought we eventually figured it out that first night, but i think the hot water just started to run out, because the next day we experienced the same boiling hot water, with *no* ability to cool it down. on that second night, Tiffany sent a repairman to fix th
items.one_line: Boiling shower triggers smoke detector, second visit same next night

items.comment:
This penthouse is stunning, to look at. However, in terms of function and location, I don't recommend it. First off, the Palms hotel is really sketchy. The other guests are sketchy and shady and I would never stay here again simply because it's in the Palms. But the problem is also with the penthouse. They try to sell it as LUXURY, however, everything is broken, so definitely not a luxurious experience.
First of all, there was urine on the pillows on the couch, literally was wet and smelled of it. I mentioned this to the host and the host didn't care, COOL, super luxurious huh.
The j
items.one_line: Urine pillows and exposed carpet nails in luxury penthouse

items.comment:
The host is completely unresponsive and they still haven't responded to my messages!!! Be aware that they require you to check-in on a separate website and they ask a lot of personal questions including your passport info.
The staircase is very narrow but that is explained so no complains about that. But be aware that it is realllly narrow! If you're a big person, there's no way you can fit in the door, let alone going up the stairs! However, I didn't expect the bathroom to be too tight. You can barely sit on the toilet and when you shower, the whole bathroom gets flooded!
They also li
items.one_line: Bathroom floods when showering; host limits showers to five minutes

items.comment:
Free Horror Movie Experience
If you’ve ever wanted to star in a real-life horror movie, this BnB is the perfect set! Picture this: two women, just having a normal conversation at a reasonable volume—no party, no loud music—yet somehow, the mysterious neighbor decided we were disturbing the peace and complains about us. But the real nightmare begins when an unknown man showed up at 12pm, ringing our doorbell repeatedly. When we asked who he is, he refused to answer clearly. Instead, he kept pressing the doorbell non-stop, turning our intercom into an unwanted live-stream of our private conv
items.one_line: Mystery man's relentless doorbell haunts night like horror film

items.comment:
The stay at this airbnb came with many pros and cons but unfortunately for us, the cons outweigh the pros. The location was great, although that was why we chose it. The Airbnb itself was indescribable.
I will give you a detailed visual and mental tour:
The front patio entrance of the space is decorated in an ensemble of trash such as a random pile up of cardboard boxes, a rusty old bike, and trashbags scattered about. As you step into the unit, you are welcomed by hardwood floors tattered in months of built up dirt and crust remains. It had a stench of old water and wood which I imagi
items.one_line: Trash patio leads to filthy floors, garbage stairway, pizza box dump

items.comment:
Don't pay any attention to the details of this apartment - based on our stay last weekend, it is a complete fabrication. They say to take your shoes off and hold down the noise to be respectful of their neighbors - WHAT A JOKE.
When we arrived the entire apartment reeked of weed - it permeated every crevice - and we later found out that it wasn't the previous tenant - it's the people living or renting the adjoining apartment. The smoked all day and all night to the point that all of our clothes smelled like pot - even when we got them home.
And this wasn't the worst of it - both nigh
items.one_line: Weed stench and raging parties featuring 100-person police bust.

items.comment: We were not lucky with a super host and family friendly listing. The first step of check-in was a bad start. We were in Disney land and I had to leave my family in the park and come to Paris just for keys collection. I could have spent an extra 5 hours in Disneyland with family, had check-in process been little flexible. Lucky homes (who were to give us key) told me to book a hotel if I am late by few minutes so I had to come in between to pick up key. We had a stroller and stairs are unusually difficult to navigate especially at night with some floors of missing light (may be sensor gone bad)
items.one_line: Forced to leave Disneyland for key, host bathroom exposed

items.comment:
Biagio is a friendly, fantastic host and detail-oriented. He explained everything very thoroughly at check-in and checked in through messaging a few times to make sure we were okay or ask if we needed anything.
The property is nice. By the end of our week-long stay, we called it "The house of 1000 LED's." You really have to get used to all of the different light switches and remotes and which lights they are for and how to find the switches you need.
The kitchen was functional with various pots, pans, utensils, electric kettle, etc. We contributed a bread knife and sharpener. :) We sh
items.one_line: Bathroom walls not glass, house of thousand LEDs

items.comment: Beds and bedding were good, location fabulous however lots of disappointments… from no toilet paper, threatening text message during our stay about not being in the courtyard after 9 and that they had noise monitors monitoring us ( which we had absolutely no intention of doing), not a frying pan to cook with in the so called ‘chefs kitchen’, taps and fixtures falling off in showers, a shabby vanity propped up on two pieces of wood, a fire in the beautiful courtyard that the owners show but don’t want you to use ( also lied about dropping wood off when we asked - after the event), dead cockroac
items.one_line: Threatening noise monitor texts, dead cockroach in dish

items.comment:
Martina is a very nice host. She was very welcoming and communicative before and during our trip. The flat is in a very good location with close links to U-Bahn, S-Bahn and shops.
The flat was initially fine, but a few issues occurred during our stay. The shower is quite old and not attached to the wall (making showering difficult) and the toilet started leaking its contents onto the floor during our stay. This made the the bathroom smell quite bad. Also, we had six people staying in the flat and the beds consisted of one double bed, a sofa bed and a set of bunk beds. This wasn't really
items.one_line: Guest blamed for neighbor's noise; police called mid-stay

items.comment:
In and Out. Never stayed in. left 45 minutes after getting in. Lost $540.00 dollars just to pay the host a free-gift for a misleading listing. . What a disgrace!! Very Bad place!!! Besides its great pictures from the listing itself and besides that it is true the inside looks accurate from the picture- nothing from that point makes sense.. Totally disappointed & angry. We booked right away the Hilton's Torres Pines within the 45 minutes after getting inside this
( Brandon's) ole investment property. And while finding homeless man sleeping by the entrance door before we called the cops
items.one_line: Homeless man at door, left 45 minutes, booked Hilton immediately

items.comment:
Do NOT stay here. First, the host is very nice and helpful. She helped arrange transport from the airport, allowed us to check in EARLY, brought us a clothes steamer and changed our money into laundry coins. Very, very great host. But, still, do not stay here.
The apartment is on the ground floor and smells of paint. Every time someone in the bldg comes in or out, you can hear it from this unit as well as water in the pipes. The bathroom is clean on first glance, but upon close inspection, it was disgusting and tiny. The sink is falling off of the wall, the shower curtain is hanging by a
items.one_line: Amazing host, haunted apartment with shower mold nightmares

items.comment:
This place is overpriced for what it is. My expectations were low, but not this low.
I do not recommend this place for people who need a quiet place to stay, and people who have OCD / care about cleanliness, those who have disabilities, and those who may worry about not getting immediate help for issues that may arise (there could be many).
Noise: This is not a quiet place. There is a preschool next door and literally right outside the big back bedroom window. Kids were screaming from about 9am until 5pm. I had all the windows shut, but I could hear them from the living room on the ot
items.one_line: Preschool torture, barking dogs, thin doors, nightmare noise

items.comment:
I've stayed in many airbnbs over the years and have never written a remotely negative review or a review longer than a few sentences.
That being said, I stayed here for three nights and every single one of those nights I was awakened by an extremely loud rooster that would crow for 10-15 minute long periods. I currently live in DC across the street from a freeway and a firehouse, so trust me when I say I am no stranger to noise and can usually sleep through just about everything. The first night was the worst, the rooster cawed every hour from 2am to 6am and I basically gave up on sleepin
items.one_line: Rooster crows every hour from 2am to 6am straight

items.comment:
Steve was a great host and the place is very centrally located. He was quick to respond which is always good.
However there was a very scary incident with a neighbor our last day/night. We had heard a woman upset and yelling earlier in the day. She was speaking in a mix of Hawaiian and English so she seemed to be a local and other tenants were talking about her. We went to bed early so we could wake up for Haleakala, but were abruptly woken up by screaming,banging and doors slamming. She was screaming and saying horrible things and we heard a fight and people were being thrown around. The
items.one_line: Neighbor attacked at night, blood splattered on walls, police called

items.comment: An overall awful nightmarish and inconvenient experience: 28 day stay at Sharon’s started with inconvenience and ended with chaos.  I arrive to a studio with frozen pipes (and flooded basement) that no one knew about or fixed before my arrival. The first 5 days in the unit I was without water and barely any heat at the peak of February wintertime with daily promises that it'd get fixed.  Sharon was on radio silence and had referred me to her husband, who warned me via text that she isn't easy to deal with .  They were both out in California in different locations and over the course of my stay
items.one_line: Frozen pipes, no water, host silent, personal belongings violated

items.comment: I am sorry to leave a bad review because the hosts were nice and responsive, but my room was quite dusty, and someone took a dump without flushing (I am not sure why and I am not blaming the hosts for this but it did have an impact on my staying experience…). I was exhausted after a long haul flight so I decided just to take a good rest and I am leaving the next morning. However when I was about to switch the light, I saw a rat on the floor and immediately hid under my bed after he had seen me. After that, I felt that I had to leave and looked for a nearby hotel. I left at around 10pm. I have
items.one_line: Unflushed poop, rat under bed, fled at 10pm

items.comment: Our overall experience with the residence was really nice. The location is very central, right next to the Santa Maria del Fiore. So if you want to enjoy the really old and precious parts of Florence, this choice is the right one - if you can find it. Because the people in florence don't have a number for each house but more like the same number for three houses, which is kind of dumb but then again each number has a different color so you'd think that shouldn't be a problem except of course that it is  because first you have to know about the fact that these guys kind of messed up numbering t
items.one_line: Florence building numbering system and Google Maps confusion odyssey

items.comment:
Unfortunately our stay in this apartment wasn’t pleasant.
Cleanliness was not up to standard - for reference, it would fail an “end of lease clean” inspection. The host has said they will change cleaners. One of the biggest things was that there was a viagra pill under the couch cushions - so obviously wasn’t being cleaned thoroughly (not to mention dangerous if small kids were to stay and find it)
The amenities were average. Unfortunately just missing the finishing touches like free sachets of coffee and sugar. The TV was missing it’s remote (host said a new one is already on its wa
items.one_line: Viagra pill under couch cushions, no remote, smoke alarm safety risk

items.comment:
Jean’s room has its pros and cons. The room was clean, beautifully decorated, and had a spacious wardrobe. Check-in was easy, and Jean was very responsive with clear instructions. However, the listing overstates privacy-the walls are thin, and we were woken both mornings by loud snot rocketing, phlegm coughing, and spitting noises from the shared bathroom. The bathroom was clean except for the visible remains of snot and blood in the sink each morning. Additionally, the outdoor photos are misleading, as furniture is stored outside.
The amenities are fine for a short stay, with a fridge, mi
items.one_line: Thin walls reveal neighbor's phlegm symphony every morning

items.comment:
The price is good BUT …
There is on-street parking (for small cars) BUT none “on-premises”
The rooms are large BUT sparsely furnished and no garden or patio
The bed is large and comfy BUT the stairs are narrow and steep
The water is hot BUT the bathroom smells of sewer gas (Carlo has been working on it)
The kitchen is functional BUT the pots are old and there isn’t a kitchen knife
The neighborhood is dodgy BUT Carlo left enough food so we didn’t have to shop
You could certainly do worse but this place doesn’t rate a 4.9 on AirBnB, maybe 4.6
items.one_line: Everything great BUT price doesn't match reality

items.comment: At first glance, the room seemed clean, but after a few days, the weather turned colder and we had to keep the windows closed for warmth. With the windows closed, we found it very hard to breathe without sneezing or coughing, and smelled a mildew odor. Upon looking around, we noticed 4 substantial patches of black mold on the walls that shared the bathroom space. After discovering this health hazard, we decided to move to a new place to stay as soon as possible. The issue with the mold has been reported to Airbnb, and they made a partial refund to me and also stated that they would check back
items.one_line: Four substantial black mold patches, health hazard, no soap provided

items.comment: NOT CHARMING-Terrible -strong-pee smell, hairs on moldy, smelly unwashed sheets, towels used and yet unwashed and mold smell, heavy choking dust clinging to drapes (with white cat hair) and  dusty smelling wall hanging and dusty cheap, rough bamboo tables—which cannot be cleaned-Cheapest Thai bed so hard it hurt my back despite my bringing a yoga mat I slept on. Dirty sink, leaking sink, stopper broken in blocked position, toilet in upper floor leaking to this stained ceiling. Washing sheets and towels helped, but wife scrubbed bath floor every 3 days and pee smell returned. Owner tried to mas
items.one_line: Bed so hard it hurt, cockroaches daily, body lice on sofa

items.comment:
I had a terrible experience.
It was while we were cooking dinner. We steamed potatoes in a pot on the burner and then were trying to fry a steak in a pan. Of course, the burner, pot, and pan were all provided by the host. When we put the pan on the burner, the burner(electrical one) did not work. The pan was not for the electrical burner! Then we used the pot. When we put the steak in the pot for about 15 seconds, the smoke alarm went off. Very surprised, we turned off the burner and tried to open the door and windows to let the smoke out. But the alarm kept sounding very loudly. We calle
items.one_line: Pan smoke, fire department arrives, host demands guest pays fee.

items.comment: This was an OK stay. The location is actually not as close to the city, if you take the F train, it will be 17 stops into the city, kinda rough travels when you’re out sight seeing all day. The room is small, perfect for two only.  A PLUS; there are also a few stores where you can get a quick breakfast (Deli Shop) or drinks/snacks. The lower level where this room is located has a weird smell, maybe a mixture of old sewer and cats. Once you get in the room, the smell isn’t as strong but still faintly present. Also, there was a ripped fake 100 dollar bill on the steps to the entrance, we left it
items.one_line: Dead baby mouse guest stays entire five-day visit

items.comment:
Wow. That’s a super bad wow, not a good wow. DO NOT STAY HERE.
I have stayed in Airbnbs many many times over the years and this place was, hands down, the WORST I’ve ever encountered. The pictures and description are inaccurate and I’m glad others have had a good experience staying at this place but boy oh boy this was not our experience!
Immediately upon walking in to the basement apartment, we noticed a strong smell of mold.
Next thing we noticed was that the beds were hastily made and there was a clump of hair attached to a wire (??) placed on the dining table. Yuck.
**T
items.one_line: Hair on wire plus spider in tub equals nightmare apartment

items.comment:
The experience I had with Mallorca Villa Selection is the worst I ever had on AirBnb.
Never seen such a bad and unprofessional attitude from a host...
I'm a regular user of AirBnb (more than 40 reservations lately) cause I work remotely (I am the founder and CEO of a Tech Company).
So having a proper WiFi connection is not an option. It's a MUST.
Of course I know when reserving a villa in the middle of an Island (Mallorca), you can't always expect having a 300Mo connection... And that's fine.
In few AirBnb I went to, I had low Wifi, but I used my mobile as a router and
items.one_line: No WiFi in remote villa forced CEO to work elsewhere.

items.comment: This rental is in a great location. You are able to walk to the french quarter to all the restaurants/attractions and it has wifi.  We were greeted by a guy who briefly introduced himself, the house, and then rushed out (we now believe it was to avoid all interaction or questions that might arise about the property). Upon further inspection, we began to recognize a consistent smell that we unanimously agreed can only be described as the remnants of a grandpa’s sneeze. The kitchen area was odd, with merely three dirty mini fridges that lack the common cold feature fridges are known for, without
items.one_line: Smells like grandpa's sneeze, three broken mini fridges, no hot water

items.comment: Don’t be deceived by the patio picture or any other staged photo. The patio is unusable due to the pigeon droppings. The droppings are down the walls and all over the floor, the smell is terrible. I would not sit outside without a hazmat suit. We did try to clean the space up with disinfectant. It made the area just a little better, so we could get some fresh air in the apartment but within a day we would have had to scrub again due to the amount of pigeon droppings. One bird was injured and trapped on the patio adding to the mess. We captured it and set it free on the street. The air conditio
items.one_line: Patio covered in pigeon droppings, injured bird trapped, oven door won't close

items.comment:
***This review is more about Evan than the apartment, we did not end up staying in the apartment booked due to reasons that Evan didn't disclose until less than 24 hours before our stay***
Giving us 15 hours notice, Evan advised that we were unable to reside in the apartment. We called Evan and he was cagey about the reason and when we pushed him he told us it was a plumbing issue and that he had another apartment in the same building that we could stay in and sent us a link https://abnb.me/SkScoGKJl1. This apartment was not nearly as nice, but with such little notice we had no other op
items.one_line: Host lied about plumbing, actually bedbugs problem

items.comment:
Big Trouble -> READ THIS
I complained to roommates who were noisy during the night to be silent. Linda heart somehow about it and wanted me to be more tolerant about them and to stop knocking on their rooms.
What you do if your direct room neighbors are up till 3am and talk and watch TV loud disturbing your sleep?
The problem persisted and after complaint attempts from my side it escalated: I filmed them to have proof and they got mad at me. One of them came with a knife 🔪 to my window!!!
I called the police 🚔 but they didn't do much.
Linda was strangely not on my side
items.one_line: Neighbor brought knife to window, used firecrackers as weapons

items.comment: Despite this house being well-appointed and comfortable with plenty of room for our two families (four adults and two toddlers), we had several very poor experiences with the property and host that unfortunately colored our impression in a negative light. The cleaning wasn’t finished until hours after the check-in time; parking information from the host was misleading, resulting in us receiving a parking ticket AND a dead rat on our car (issues with which the host was unhelpful in resolving); the property was not very safe/kid-friendly; and basic amenities were either difficult to find or not
items.one_line: Dead rat on car plus cleaning delays equals Tokyo parking disaster

items.comment:
This bnb is in a great location, it’s really well decorated & designed, small, but super close to the beach & lots of restaurants/cafes, & was very clean.
However, we stayed here for 10 nights, & we felt as though it lacked some very basic essentials that would have made our stay 5 stars:
-more than 1 set of sheets
-more than 1 set of towels (only 1 per person & only 1 hand towel)
-no doors anywhere, so no privacy (including no bathroom door which is directly next to the bed, so if ur not close to ur partner maybe not the place for u!!!)
-no paper towel in the kitchen and
items.one_line: Beautiful beachside location: no privacy doors, cockroach in kitchen discovered

items.comment:
According to my own experience, I did not like my residence at Fred’s apartment.
Pros:
A lot of coffee, a fully equipped kitchen and bathroom, a clean house, and a nice host.
Cons:
Very very very bad neighbors upstairs. They made our experience so terrible… They made much noise any time… after midnight, during the day, disturbing my baby/family's sleep and giving us hard time. Fred talked to them twice but there were no remarkable changes with the fire alarm turning on at 1:00 AM, saying bad words just in front of our main door (which is 4 feet away from our bed lol) day after
items.one_line: Upstairs suite has no door, neighbors sing, dance, swear constantly

items.comment: This place was great considering how close it was to the beach which wasn’t crowded at all. The house did sleep our group of 10 comfortably; however, in regards to the house, there were a few flaws that we were not expecting. The house is very nice looking but seems to have to been finished cheaply. The shower door handles and drains were broken, the garage code did not work from the outside, and the upstairs shower leaked water through the ceiling all over the bed in the bedroom directly below. Lastly, the biggest problem we had was that there were noise detectors up on the roof. The rule was
items.one_line: Noise detector called at 3am when neighbors were loud

items.comment:
PLEASE READ BEFORE RENTING THIS PLACE!!! If you like to sleep DO NOT GO HERE!!!!
We rented this place not realizing that the appartment is literally in the FAVELA ! You can hear and see everything!!! There is no note of this in the description.
All hours of the day you will hear blaring music, yelling, screaming and of course roosters cockaling all throughout the night. SO LOUD!!!!!
We were even lucky to see and hear a GUN fight, YIKES!!!!!
Needless to say we did not stay for our full reservation, as we came for a fun relaxing holiday not a favela music festival experience.
items.one_line: Located in favela, witnessed gunfight, owner suggests buying earplugs

items.comment: Great location, but place was filthy. Not secure. Duct tape was used for plumbing and electrical instead of having professionals fixing things correctly. Bedding not clean. Toilet runs 24/7 because some genius hooked it up to a hot water line. Sprinklers, fire alarms, and the dead bolt on the entry door covered in duct tape to make them work-ish.  Roach spray thoughtfully  provided for bugs. Towels are ragged and have holes in them. Shower faucets are old, rusted, and don't work as they should. I never pan hosts, check my previous reviews, but this host comments in previous reviews that this p
items.one_line: Duct tape plumbing, toilet runs 24/7, roach spray provided

items.comment: In all my years of using air bnb, this has been the single most disgusting place I have ever had the misfortune of being in. As soon as I stepped in, there was no fire alarm. Wires just hanging from the ceiling as if the whole thing had been ripped out. I went to the bathroom and not only was there no toilet paper but I was greeted by a cockroach. After using the restroom (I couldn’t wait anymore or I would’ve) I went to the couch to communicate with the host. BLOOD STAINS on the couch and pillow. This caused me to go check the beds. Not only were there blood stains on the mattress, there was
items.one_line: No fire alarm, cockroach in bathroom, blood stains on bed and couch

items.comment:
This accommodation facilities are very old and poor, a fake picture of swimming pool which very small. And there are no Air conditioning in living room and one of room, how can stay there in hot weather? toilet is dirty and with many moustiques and cockroaches in the house!!! super dirty and disgusting!!! we have horrible problems happened at last night. (we booked for 3 days but we just stay for a 1-2 hours only then check out AT 3AM)
On 28june, we check-in FAB house around 10:30pm when there were some questionable strangers (perhaps junkies) walking around the main gate area before we in
items.one_line: Junkies outside, checkouts at 3am, intruders in house area

items.comment:
I stayed here for five days, and where to start? :)
1) Location: okay, let's just say this, because no host (no matter how good) would advertise this fact, but it's like 200 meters from a towering yellow prison. That out of the way, should that deter you? No. First off, from the cottage, it's only visible if you *try* to see it, and only barely, through one window; the rest of the scenery is lovely and rustic, a very old-world aesthetic. Secondly, the odds that while you're there, there's going to be a prison break, *and* the prisoners are going to choose to hide out in your cottage *righ
items.one_line: Cottage near prison; host reassures about prison break risk

items.comment:
The place is beautiful! Very nice & looks just like the pictures. There were a few hiccups so just beware:
-The owner lives upstairs, which is fine. BUT it’s very inconsiderate to have extremely loud sex knowing you have guests. I had to blast music to not hear it, but luckily he got the hint and kept it down afterwards. I shouldn’t have to do that though. Just don’t want to have to hear OBNOXIOUSLY loud sex sounds while I’m trying to enjoy vacation. Almost thought it was a prank because it was so loud.
-There was a hot water issue in the master shower, he came to fix it but it would n
items.one_line: Owner's loud sex required music blasting to mask it

items.comment:
Don’t be fooled by the positive reviews which are just an act of kindness in return to the very kind employees at the bar, but it is not based on the actual stay.
I stayed five days, during which a guest had his belongings stolen, good way to spend time at the police station. There is a balcony, when it is open, two rooms of eight people each are connected via the balcony, so it means that fifteen other people can come in your room to steal your stuff. The price of the dorm bed adds up to almost being the price of a private room in a regular hotel because you have to pay for your towel and
items.one_line: Guest theft, fifteen people can enter your room

items.comment: Loft was charming and in a fantastic location, but had very thin floor and walls. We heard everything that was happening in the neighboring unit, and unfortunately, it was occupied by a large group of guys who were LOUDLY coming and going through the wee hours of the morning. The entrance to the upstairs unit is right next to the entrance of this unit, and unfortunately, one of the guys actually entered our unit late at night one evening. What was alarming was that the other unit's door code actually worked on our unit. When we asked the host about this, he initially accused us of leaving the
items.one_line: Drunk neighbor uses wrong door code, enters unit late at night

items.comment:
BEWARE!
Price of the listing was great and the location was amazing. At first upon check in, the apartment looked clean and stylish, however once we settled in, we found many issues. The place was not cleaned properly. There were several cracks in the floorboards that created space for dust and dirt to build up. There were no paper towels. The TV was not working. When we messaged the host, they told us to plug it in another outlet. We obviously tried that and it didn't work, so we just left it.
BUT the biggest issue we had, which was completely unacceptable and utterly disgusting, was
items.one_line: Two-inch cockroach on dresser, unresponsive host

items.comment:
My friend and I have been staying at airbnb houses for many years but this is the first time that we have been literally kicked out of the house in the middle of the night! And we haven't yet understood the reason why this happened.
My friend and I were travelling to London and I had booked this room to stay on my own. My friend had a flight the next day early in the morning and instead of letting her roam on the streets during the night, I asked her to stay with me for a few hours at the room that I had booked. She would stay for no more that 5 hours at the room, so I thought that the ho
items.one_line: Kicked out midnight for friend staying five hours

items.comment: Let me start saying that it breaks my heart to give four stars to this beautiful place. The house is fantastic, being right on the ocean is an exquisite pleasure, communication with the host is impeccable, and really whatever depends on the owners is absolutely perfect. But there is one problem. The neighbor. The first morning I woke up to a beautiful sunset accompanied by non-stop dog barking and howling. After about an hour of nonstop barking and howling (and I mean loud and nonstop, not a second of a break) I asked the neighbor if anything can be done: maybe dogs can be brought inside or on
items.one_line: Neighbor's dogs bark nonstop, threatens guest coldly

items.comment:
I did not get the apartment that is in the pictures. The pictures show a beautiful, bright, clean apartment with wood wrapped windows and wood floors., and lovely decorated bedrooms. The pictures are a dream! However, the apartment I received was not this apartment at all, and not just redecorated, it was not the same apartment. In fact, I honestly thought I had walked into a neighbors apartment by accident. It looked like someone lived there and would return any moment. I messaged the host to make sure I wasn’t trespassing. He thought it was funny that I would be so confused.
I can
items.one_line: Wrong apartment entirely, moldy coffee and rotten flowers

items.comment: I've never complained about lodging because usually, you know what you're getting. I rented this room hoping for a nice stay the night before having to drive all the way back to KOA airport. The check-in staff was very pleasant and the waterfall was nice, however, the room was not adequate. The room smelled like urine, but it was subtle so bearable. Then about midnight, I was woken up by water leaking on my face. There was a light rain outside, the usual Hilo spray, but the majority of the room was soaked. There were 2 LARGE puddles on the floor, one of which soaked through one of my suitcases
items.one_line: Midnight leak drips directly on guest's face during sleep

items.comment: All in all it was an okay stay. The location is very good, the 24 hour doorman services are very good. We had issues with the water heater and they sent a technician to fix it. The neighbors were extremely annoying, complaining about our dog barking, while being super loud very late at night. Thankfully the host was understanding and ready to accept our side of the situation rather than just take the neighbors' complaints as truth. However, the bathroom faucet was leaking water, it never got replaced. There was a small table in the living room, half broken, ended up breaking on my fiancés foot
items.one_line: Dog complaints, broken faucet, stuck elevator, ant infestation

items.comment: Marie was great with check-in and communication responses to resolve anything she could. However, the listing details don’t seem to read as accurate as they need to be and I wanted to leave a review for others to make sure they are aware. The listing does say it’s a shared living space and bathroom, so we knew that going in, however, the bathroom is shared between 3 other rooms, which means the one bathroom could be shared between 6 adults, IF the rooms had 2 people each, which they are meant to, but there were times over our long stay that more than 2 adults were in a room. That being said, e
items.one_line: Husband forced to pee in bottle when bathroom locked during night

items.comment: Well. I usually won't leave a review if I don't have a great time. This however I feel needs an explanation.  First of all I don't understand how this has so many great reviews. My girlfriend and I aren't overly picky or demanding but these reviews seem entirely inaccurate. Lets start with the positives.  Beautiful location in the Topanga mountains and the one owner we briefly met was very friendly. Lets move on to the rest. The "Casita" is tiny. The pictures are deceiving.  The bed nearly touches three out of the four walls and sitting on the bed I could touch the 4th. I'm 6'1" and the bed is
items.one_line: Bed so tiny guest's feet stick through frame, shower floods toilet

items.comment:
“If you are not happy with the place, you can go somewhere else. We are not giving refunds or anything. The people came today to do a deep cleaning...Sorry for the trash and dirty blankets.” Not recommended!!! I have traveled many places and used Airbnb’s and this by far has been the worst experience. Place looks very similar to the pictures minus the first room being a lot smaller than shown.
The place was NOT clean. The concierge ensured the placed was deep cleaned by four people earlier that day, however, I would say otherwise.The linen had stains in both rooms, the bathroom had what a
items.one_line: Go somewhere else, we won't refund, roaches on walls

items.comment:
Don't be deceived by the 4 Star ratings. READ THE REVIEWS in detail and you will find the truth about this place. "Cockroaches" ... "No Parking - Parked in for 2 hours" ... "Cleanliness not great" ... "Hear music all hours" ... "Hot" ... "Three flights of stairs" ... "Informed me after that there was a $30 pet fee".
I understand that people want to be nice, but posting good reviews for dishonest Hosts with dilapidated, stinking accommodations does a disservice to other AirBnB users who end up with a terrible experience, like we had.
Here is the truth about this place (and my first nega
items.one_line: Cockroaches, mold stench, three flights of stairs.

items.comment:
Safe neighborhood close to tourist locations
BUT
When we arrived in the back alley. We were greeted with a live and dead rat by the big green dumpster. Complicated instructions instead of clearly identifying the garage door (or sending a picture) made the place hard to find
I asked about several things including the hot tub. Benjamin responded to all my inquiries but omitted the tub-related ones. We asked the housekeeper about it and she looked puzzled. I then got a message from Benjamin stating that the hot tub was removed the week prior. I doubt this because there is winterized
items.one_line: Dead rat at entrance; moldy fridge; rat trap under bed

items.comment:
Have you ever thought you were in paradise and then you realized it could get even better? Enter Jamie and Erin’s Contemporary Condo (or as I like the call it, Jamie and Erin’s CC by the Sea or the CCSea [patent pending]).
Honestly, I am one of the hardest people to impress ESPECIALLY when it comes to housing (for I’ve had many a bad experiences in the past) however I’m happy to say this was the best vacation find I have found so far.
Long story short, I visited Hawaii for a graduation trip and I, having this curse of wanting to live like royalty, assigned myself to select the sleepin
items.one_line: Royalty expectations meet North Shore reality check

items.comment:
I think Jonathan is a highly responsive host who cares about his property.
First and foremost, the house is right next to (as in, only a couple of steps away from) active train tracks. I’m shocked that no one has mentioned this in prior reviews; it’s a huge deal but it isn’t mentioned anywhere. I couldn’t sleep at all. The house shakes whenever a train passes and it’s very loud. I’m a light sleeper so the noise and constant movement woke me up throughout the night and it was very hard for me to even fall asleep. In addition, there’s some type of loud buzzing noise that happens every half
items.one_line: House next to active train tracks; shaking and roaring nightly

items.comment: This apartment is lovely, nicely appointed with great dishes and cutlery, something that you don’t always find in rental apartments. It also had nice appliances and the view is beautiful. Having said that, we wouldn’t stay here again and due to no fault of the host but the location. First, you’re in the direct flight path of the airport which is no super big deal because the planes will eventually stop at night but the bigger issue is they are located right next to a park that has free range roosters and peacocks. Dozens of these loud birds. Those birds start making noise at 2 AM, not kidding.
items.one_line: Roosters and peacocks screech starting at midnight continuously
